comment: thinkBox strict seccomp profile — blocks dangerous syscalls for sandboxed code execution

syscalls.comment: Block mount/unmount — prevent filesystem namespace manipulation
Block system reboot/shutdown — prevent host disruption
Block process tracing — prevent debugging/injection attacks
Block eBPF — prevent kernel-level manipulation
Block userfaultfd — prevent kernel exploit primitives
Block io_uring — prevent async I/O kernel exploitation
Block root filesystem pivot — prevent container escape
Block namespace manipulation — prevent container escape
Block kernel module loading — prevent rootkit injection
Block swap management — prevent host memory manipulation
Block system clock manipulation — prevent time-based attacks
Block raw I/O port access — prevent hardware access
Block syslog access — prevent log manipulation/info leak
Block kernel key management — prevent credential theft
Block personality changes — prevent execution mode bypass
Block acct — prevent process accounting manipulation
Block move_pages/mbind — prevent NUMA memory policy manipulation
Block vhangup/nfsservctl — prevent tty/NFS exploits
Block lookup_dcookie — prevent kernel memory info leak
Block perf_event_open — prevent performance counter abuse
